architecture.summary: A sequence-to-sequence model with LSTM encoder-decoder and attention that accepts linearized tree-structured meaning representations (MRs) as input and generates linearized tree-structured response annotations as output. Information flows from tree-structured semantic input through standard Seq2Seq attention mechanisms to constrained beam search decoding that validates output trees against input tree constraints.

biblio.title: Constrained Decoding for Neural NLG from Compositional Representations in Task-Oriented Dialogue

claims.statement: S2S-Constr achieves tree accuracy of 99.25 on E2E dataset, compared to S2S-Tree at 94.86
S2S-Constr achieves tree accuracy of 96.92 on weather dataset, compared to S2S-Tree at 92.5
On challenging E2E subset with contrasts, S2S-Constr achieves 66.09% semantic correctness compared to S2S-Flat at 30.87%
On challenging weather subset with discourse relations, S2S-Constr achieves 93.44% semantic correctness compared to S2S-Flat at 79.04%
S2S-Constr achieves more than 90% tree accuracy with just 2K training samples and more than 95% with 5K samples on both E2E and weather datasets

Constrained decoding enforces that opening bracket tokens are accepted only if they are children of the current parent node in the input tree and have not already been generated, and closing bracket or EOS tokens are accepted only if all children of the current parent are covered
S2S-Constr fails to generate any valid candidates for ~1.5% of the weather test examples, with the model stuttering and producing degenerate output

Tree-structured MRs allow models to leverage learned discourse relations for domain adaptation, with a model trained on weather data and flat E2E data achieving 76% tree accuracy on compositional E2E examples with only 5% of compositional examples added to training
